goal: Size a GPU cluster for 2000-env IsaacLab Franka training within $400k capex or cloud equivalent.
thoughts: Envs-per-GPU is workload-dependent — give a defensible range (100-500 for Franka pick/place on H100/L40S) but ALWAYS qualify as 'measure your workload'. High-res camera observations shift bottleneck from compute to VRAM bandwidth — L40S (48 GB) may beat L4 (24 GB) for camera-heavy workloads. NVLink/NVSwitch matters for PPO with large critic syncs; for embarrassingly-parallel IsaacLab, per-GPU independence is fine. Compare capex vs cloud honestly. Never quote exact cloud prices (stale).
code:
# 1. GPU class candidates
lookup_product_spec(product="NVIDIA L40S")
lookup_product_spec(product="NVIDIA H100 SXM")
lookup_product_spec(product="NVIDIA RTX 6000 Ada")
lookup_knowledge(query="isaaclab franka pick place envs per gpu h100 l40s benchmark")

# 2. Small measurement run — 64 envs, 1 GPU, 10-min profile
run_small = launch_training(
    env="Isaac-Franka-PickPlace",
    num_envs=64,
    steps=10000,
    gpu="<GPU_SKU>",
    dry_run=False,
    profile=True,
)
thru = profile_training_throughput(run_id=run_small.id)
vram = check_vram_headroom(run_id=run_small.id)

# 3. Extrapolate to 2000 envs with measured envs/GPU
# n_gpus = ceil(2000 / envs_per_gpu_measured)
# round up to node size (8 x L40S per server typical)

# 4. Compare capex vs cloud
cloud_estimate_cost(
    workload="isaaclab_franka_2000env",
    steps_per_env=500000,
    gpu_class="<GPU_SKU>",
    # DO NOT hardcode $/hr numbers — tool pulls current rates
)

# 5. Interconnect decision
lookup_knowledge(query="isaaclab ppo critic sync nvlink nvswitch embarrassingly parallel")
failure_modes: assistant quotes envs/GPU number without qualifying as 'measure yours'
assistant ignores VRAM headroom for camera-heavy variant
assistant says 'just use L4' without checking VRAM
